Container.Name: media-purge
Container.Overview: Rule-based media cleanup for Plex and Jellyfin. Scans your libraries, recommends what to delete (never watched, watched years ago, duplicates, low quality, dead series...) with a transparent explanation for every suggestion, and reclaims space through a staged pipeline: approve -> recycle bin -> purge after retention. Ships dry-run enabled so nothing is deleted until you decide you trust it. Also includes ImageMaid-style server maintenance (Plex PhotoTranscoder cache purge, Clean Bundles / Optimize DB, Jellyfin cache tasks). Every action is written to a permanent activity log.

Config.Name: Media
Config.Description: Your media share. Must be read-write for cleanup to work. Add a path mapping in Settings if your media server mounts this share at a different path.

Config.Name: Recycle Bin
Config.Description: Where approved deletions wait out the retention window. Keep it on the SAME share as your media so moves are instant instead of copies.

Config.Name: Plex Appdata (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: mount your Plex appdata to enable the PhotoTranscoder cache purge (often frees 50-100+ GB). Point Settings -> Appdata paths at /plex-appdata/Library/Application Support/Plex Media Server.

Config.Name: Jellyfin Appdata (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: mount your Jellyfin config dir (the one containing cache/) to enable the on-disk cache purge with dry-run size estimates. Point Settings -> Appdata paths at /jellyfin-appdata.
